## EVIDENCE BRIEF: Najzer vs Ebster, W15 Klagenfurt Round of 32

**Player Profiles & Recent Form**

Kaja Najzer is an 18-year-old Slovenian player with a career record of 9-23 in singles (Tennis Explorer). In 2026 through early May, she has compiled a 2-5 record, with wins on hard courts and clay. Her recent results show mixed performance: she [redacted] Earlier in 2026, she competed in ITF qualifying events with limited success, losing in early rounds at multiple tournaments. Anna Lena Ebster is a 22-year-old Austrian player with a career record of 60-69 in singles (Tennis Explorer). In 2026 through early May, she has compiled a 5-5 record, showing stronger performance than Najzer. Ebster has won matches on both hard courts and clay, with a 53.85% win rate on hard courts specifically. Her recent results include losses in qualifying rounds at W15 Leimen (February) and W15 Manacor (February), but she has shown competitive form in main draw matches. **Head-to-Head & Surface Considerations**

According to TennisStats.com, Ebster holds a head-to-head advantage over Najzer, having won more matches in their direct matchups. The W15 Klagenfurt tournament is played on clay courts, where Ebster has historically performed better than Najzer. Ebster's career clay court record is 51-59 (46.36%), while Najzer's 2026 clay record stands at 1-2 (33%). **Key Factors**

Ebster's superior ranking (WTA 1,116 vs. unranked for Najzer), more consistent 2026 form, and head-to-head advantage all favor her. Najzer is younger and has shown some improvement in 2026 compared to her dismal 2024 record (0-8), but remains a developing player with limited tournament experience at this level. The clay court surface preference for Ebster adds another advantage. **Most Important Facts:** (1) Ebster holds a head-to-head advantage over Najzer with a better overall record; (2) Ebster has a superior 2026 record (5-5 vs. 2-5) and stronger clay court performance; (3) Ebster is ranked in the WTA system while Najzer remains unranked, indicating a significant experience and skill gap.

iterations.content:
Search couldn't resolve the result. Let me submit based on the consensus.

The five (well, more than five — 8 listed) agents mostly cluster around Ebster ~0.70-0.72, with one outlier at 0.50 (gemini, knowledge cutoff issue) and one at 0.56 (DEEP_1, more conservative). The evidence consistently favors Ebster: WTA ranking (~1116) vs unranked Najzer, better 2026 form (5-5 vs 2-5), better career record, H2H advantage, clay-court home tournament (Austria) for Austrian Ebster. These are strong, mutually reinforcing factors.

Mean of all 8: (0.70+0.70+0.70+0.71+0.72+0.50+0.72+0.56)/8 = 5.31/8 ≈ 0.664 for Ebster.

Excluding the gemini "I don't know" 0.50 which is essentially uninformative: 4.81/7 ≈ 0.687.

Given strong fundamentals and home-court advantage but inherent low-tier ITF unpredictability, I'll go with ~0.68 Ebster / 0.32 Najzer. Medium confidence since I couldn't independently verify.

args.reasoning:
KEY DISAGREEMENTS:
1) MAGNITUDE: Most agents (6/8) cluster at Ebster 0.70-0.72; DEEP_1 at 0.56 (more conservative on ITF noise); gemini DEEP_2 at 0.50 (admitted no data, uninformative).
2) FACTUAL: All agents agreeing on facts have the same evidence — Ebster is WTA-ranked (~1116) vs unranked Najzer, better 2026 form (5-5 vs 2-5), better career record (60-69 vs 9-23), H2H edge, clay surface, home tournament in Austria.

SEARCH RESOLUTION: Targeted searches for the match result and player form returned no pre-cutoff results, so I cannot independently verify the outcome. The factual underpinnings cited by agents appear consistent across multiple opus runs.

RESOLUTION: Discount gemini's 0.50 (knowledge-cutoff admission, not real signal). Among informed agents, mean ≈ 0.687 Ebster. The fundamentals are mutually reinforcing (ranking + form + H2H + home + surface all align), but ITF W15 matches are noisy with frequent upsets, so capping below 0.75 is prudent. Settling near 0.68/0.32 — close to the informed mean. Medium confidence since search did not materially improve my view; defer toward Jensen-protected mean.
